original: Yorktown, Virginia founded
Champion of England titleholder Jack Broughton publishes "Rules of the Ring," the earliest boxing code
Dutch East Indiaman The Geldermalsen sails to the East Indies
American militiamen defeat British forces at the Battle of Bennington near Vermont, during the American Revolutionary War
British decisively defeat Americans in Battle of Camden, South Carolina
Turkey declares war on Russia (Russo-Turkish War 1787-92)
Hungarian revolutionary Ignác Martinovics is arrested in Vienna
Comet C/1797 P1 (Bouvard-Herschel) approaches 0.0879 AU of Earth
Peterloo Massacre, Manchester, England: cavalry charges demonstrators, 15 people killed and 400–700 injured
Siamese twins Chang and Eng Bunker arrive in Boston to be exhibited
Skirmishes in Fredericktown/Kirkville, Missouri
Chickamauga campaign of the US Civil War begins in Georgia
Federal assault on 4th day of battle at Deep Bottom Run, Virginia
Palace for People's Industry officially opens in Amsterdam
Restoration Day in the Dominican Republic: The Dominican Republic regains its independence after four years of fighting against the Spanish annexation
Fred Goldsmith demonstrates that the curveball is not an optical illusion
British forces under General Wolseley land in Alexandria
Dutch soccer club FC Dordrecht is established in Dordrecht in the western Netherlands
Alexander Clark, journalist and lawyer, named minister to Liberia
Indian chiefs from the Sioux and Onondaga tribes meet to urge their people to renounce Christianity and return to their traditional faith
Gold is first discovered in the Klondike at Bonanza Creek in Yukon, Canada, by George Carmack
Edwin Prescott patents the first vertical loop-the-loop roller coaster
Mbunga Rebellion occupies German post Ifakara, East Africa
8.6 earthquake destroys Valparaiso, Chile; fire kills 20,000
Mulay Hafid is proclaimed Sultan of Morocco by supporters, leading to civil war; Mulay is supported by Germany, while France supports the existing Sultan
Tōhoku Imperial University of Japan (modern-day Tōhoku University) admits its first female students
German army occupies the last fort at Liège, Belgian General Leman captured
World War I: Battle of Cer begins
Kansas City Packers' Alex Main no-hits Buffalo Blues (Federal League), 5-0
US troops overrun at Archangelsk by Bolshevik troops
Cleveland Indians shortstop Ray Chapman is hit in the head by NY Yankees pitcher Carl Mays and dies the next day in the only MLB game-related fatality
Members of the Dáil (parliament) swear allegiance to the Irish Republic at their first meeting, held at Dublin's Mansion House
AT&T radio station WBAY becomes WEAF (NYC)
Conference on German recovery payments opens in London
Dutch-Turkish peace treaty is signed
Belgian cyclist Georges Ronsse wins men's road race at UCI Road World Championships in Budapest, Hungary
First British Empire Games open in Hamilton, Canada
The first color sound cartoon "Fiddlesticks" is released by Ub Iwerks (formerly of Walt Disney Studios)
US explorer William Beebe descends 3,028 feet (923 meters) in a Bathysphere off the coast of Bermuda
The Berlin Olympic equestrian competition concludes with Germany winning all 6 gold medals in an unprecedented team achievement
XI Summer Olympic Games close at Olympiastadion in Berlin, Germany
Forty-five German aircraft are shot down over England
HMS Mercury, the Royal Navy Signals School and Combined Signals School, opens at Leydene near Petersfield, England
First Long Tom bombs on Italian mainland from Sicily
2nd Canadian Division occupies Falaise, Normandy, during Operation Intractable
The Dutch begin diplomatic contact with the Vatican in London
US 15th Army Corps reaches Eure and surrounds Dreux, France
Direct Action Day: Widespread riots erupt in Calcutta between Muslims and Hindus over whether Pakistan should be a separate state, killing over 4,000 and leaving 100,000 homeless
Arabs blow up the Latrun pumping station in Jerusalem
Israeli pound becomes legal tender
West Indies complete historic 3-1 series victory over England
KTAL TV channel 6 in Shreveport-Texarkana, LA (NBC) begins broadcasting
200 pilgrims drown in Farahzad, Iran, during a flood caused by a rainstorm
American brand management company Authentic Brands Group first publishes sports magazine "Sports Illustrated"
Fiat Motors orders the first private atomic reactor
Adlai E. Stevenson is nominated as the US Democratic presidential candidate
MLB Cleveland Indians'Rocky Colavitohits his first grand slam, Cleveland 5, Tigers 4
USSR introduces installment buying
Britain grants independence to the Crown Colony of Cyprus
Joseph Kittinger parachutes from a balloon at 31,330 m (102,800 ft)
250,000 West Berliners demonstrate against the division of Berlin
Independence is restored to the Dominican Republic
Mildred Sampson of New Zealand runs the female world marathon record of 3:19:33 in Auckland, New Zealand
St. Louis Cardinals center fielderCurt Floodgets eight straight hits in a doubleheader split against the Los Angeles Dodgers at Dodger Stadium
Colgems Records releases the Monkees' debut single "Last Train to Clarksville" with Micky Dolenz singing lead on the tune written by Tommy Boyce and Bobby Hart
The Beatles perform in concert at JFK Stadium in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, for their second and final show in the city
WFIQ TV channel 36 in Florence, AL (PBS) begins broadcasting
V.V. Giri is elected the fourth President of India
WATL TV channel 36 in Atlanta, GA begins broadcasting
Over 8,000 workers go on strike in Derry, Northern Ireland, in protest against the introduction of internment, which allows suspected terrorists to be indefinitely detained without trial
Philip Potter is appointed Secretary-General of the World Council of Churches
USSR performs a nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeastern Kazakhstan
St. Louis Cardinals beat San Diego Chargers 20-10 in Tokyo, Japan (NFL Expo)
US performs a nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Yankees blow a 9-4 lead in the 9th but beat Chicago 11-10 in the bottom of the 9th
British rock keyboardist Jools Holland quits band Squeeze to pursue a solo career
Cozy Powell quits Rainbow
American swimmer Mary T. Meagher breaks her own world women's 100 m butterfly record (57.93) in Brown Deer, Wisconsin, and holds the 100/200 m double world record
Highest score in World Cup soccer match (Australia-31, American Samoa-0)
A federal jury in Los Angeles acquits automaker John Z. DeLorean of cocaine charges
Largest harness racing purse ($2,161,000—Nihilator wins $1,080,500)
NASA launches AMPTE
Sunken liner Andrea Doria's safe is opened
Sudanese rebels shoot down a Fokker F-27, killing 57 people
Astrological Harmonic Convergence - Dawn of a New Age
New York Mets beat Chicago Cubs 23-10 at Wrigley Field, Chicago; Greg Maddux records the loss
Northwest Airlines Flight 255 crashes in Detroit; 156 die, 1 survives
IBM introduces software for artificial intelligence
A solar flare from the Sun creates a geomagnetic storm that affects microchips, leading to a halt in all trading on Toronto's stock exchange
Roger Kingdom of the USA sets the 110 m hurdle record (12.92) in Zurich, Switzerland
China performs a nuclear test at Lop Nur, PRC
Iraq orders 4,000 Britons and 2,500 Americans in Kuwait to Iraq
Belgian census shows 10,000,963 inhabitants
Moses Kiptanui of Kenya runs a world 3,000 m record of 7:28.96 in Cologne, Germany
South Africa relinquishes sovereignty over Walvis Bay
The Debian distribution first announced by Ian Murdock, a student at Purdue University (Murdock initially called his system the "Debian Linux Release")
Chandrika Kumaratunga's People's Alliance wins a closely fought parliamentary election in Sri Lanka, securing 105 seats
For only the second time, the Stanley Cup leaves North America and heads to Russia

New Zealand beats Australia 36-24 in Dunedin to retain the Tri Nations Rugby Series with an unbeaten record; All Blacks flyhalf Carlos Spencer lands five penalties and three conversions; halftime NZ 36-0
The South African soccer team, Bafana Bafana wins against the national team of the Democratic Republic of the Congo at First National Bank stadium, Johannesburg, South Africa
"Hanging by a Moment" single released by Lifehouse (Billboard Song of the Year 2001)
Africa Women's Peace Train leaves Kampala for Johannesburg
New Zealand beats Australia 21-17 at Eden Park, Auckland, to win its 5th Tri Nations Rugby Series; the All Blacks have an undefeated 4-0 record and regain the Bledisloe Cup from the Wallabies

US Representative from South Dakota Bill Janklow hits and kills a motorcyclist with his car at a rural intersection near Trent, South Dakota; he is eventually convicted of manslaughter and resigns from Congress
Australian swimmer Ian Thorpe wins the men's 200 m freestyle in an Olympic record time of 1:44.71, achieving the 200/400 m freestyle double at the Athens Olympics
West Caribbean Airways Flight 708 crashes near Machiques, Venezuela, killing all 160 aboard
British cyclist Bradley Wiggins becomes the first rider to successfully defend his Olympic title in the individual pursuit when he takes gold at the Beijing Olympics
British swimmer Rebecca Adlington wraps up the 400/800 m freestyle double at the Beijing Olympics with a world record of 8:14.10 in the 800

British team of Tom James, Steve Williams, Pete Reed, and Andrew Triggs Hodge win the men's rowing coxless fours in Beijing, the third consecutive Olympics in which Great Britain wins gold in this event
César Cielo wins the men's 50 m freestyle in 21.30 seconds in Beijing, earning Brazil's first-ever Olympic gold medal in swimming
Kirsty Coventry of Zimbabwe swims a world record 2:05.24 to beat American Margaret Hoelzer by 1.01 seconds and win the 200 m backstroke at the Beijing Olympics
New Zealand rowers Caroline and Georgina Evers-Swindell successfully defend their 2004 Olympic title in the women's double sculls by beating Germany by 0.01 seconds at the Beijing Games
Romanian rower Georgeta Andrunache wins the women's coxless pairs at the Beijing Olympics, earning her fifth career gold medal and her third consecutive Olympic gold in the event

South Korean weightlifter Jang Mi-Ran wins the women's unlimited (+75 kg) division at the Beijing Olympics by breaking world records five times: once in snatch, twice in clean and jerk, and twice in total
World Youth Day 2011, organized by the Catholic Church, begins in Madrid
113 people are killed and over 200 wounded in a series of attacks across Iraq
South African police open fire on striking mine workers and kill at least 34 people
61 people die after the ferry MV Thomas Aquinas sinks in the Philippines
"Batman" Samaritan Lenny B. Robinson is killed in a car accident in Maryland
Croatian discus thrower Sandra Perković retains her Olympic title with a distance of 69.21 m at the Rio de Janeiro Games
Georgian weightlifter Lasha Talakhadze sets a world record of 473 total kilograms to win the men's +105 kg gold medal at the Rio de Janeiro Olympics
New Zealand canoeist Lisa Carrington retains her K-1 200 m Olympic title with a time of 39.864 seconds at the Rio de Janeiro Games
Russian pair Natalia Ishchenko and Svetlana Romashina retain their Olympic synchronized swimming duet title at the Rio de Janeiro Games with a final score of 194.9910
Wildfire breaks out in San Bernardino County, California, engulfing 15,000 acres and prompting the evacuation of 82,000 people
Baltimore City Council removes Confederate statues under the cover of darkness in response to Charlottesville violence
Last batch of FARC rebel weapons is removed by the UN in Colombia
Lebanon's parliament abolishes Article 522, which exempts rapists from prosecution if they marry their victim
Over 100 Maasai houses are destroyed in Tanzania by game park authorities as part of a border dispute near Serengeti National Park
Three suicide bombers kill 27 people outside a refugee camp near Maiduguri in Borno State, Nigeria; Boko Haram is suspected
Brandt Snedeker shoots 10th sub-60 round in PGA history with an 11-under par 59 in the opening round of the Wyndham Championship in Greensboro, North Carolina
British Columbia, Canada, declares a state of emergency with 566 wildfires burning, prompting the evacuation of 3,000 people
Brooke Raboutouwins a gold medal in lead climbing at the IFSC Youth World Championships in Moscow, Russia
Unprecedented flooding and landslides in Kerala, India, are reported to have killed 106 people, leaving 150,000 homeless
World's first floating dairy farm opens in Merwehaven Harbour, Rotterdam, Netherlands, with 40 cows milked by robots
Huge fire in Chalantika slum in Dhaka, Bangladesh, destroys 1,200 houses and leaves up to 10,000 people homeless
At least 17 people are killed in an attack on a beach resort in Mogadishu, Somalia, by the al-Shabab group
Biggest protests in Thailand in six years as 10,000 people demonstrate in Bangkok for reform of the government and monarchy
Japan's economy, the world's third largest, posts its worst-ever decline, falling 7.8% in the April-June quarter
First Marvel film with an Asian superhero, "Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings," starring Simu Liu, premieres in Los Angeles
First official water shortage declared at Lake Mead, a major reservoir on the Colorado River supplying 40 million people, with cuts mandated mainly for Arizona
Malaysian Prime Minister Muhyiddin Yassin and his cabinet resign after losing their majority amid political turmoil

Philadelphia Phillies' 11-4 win over the visiting Cincinnati Reds makes them the 9th MLB team with 10,000 franchise victories (and the last of those founded in the 19th century); they were the first to lose 10,000 in 2007
Sections of China's Yangtze River launch weather modification programs due to severe drought conditions, including seeding clouds with silver iodide rods to induce rain
US PresidentJoe Bidensigns the Inflation Reduction Act into law with $370 billion of spending and tax cuts to combat climate change
British Museum reveals a staff member has been dismissed for stealing about 2,000 objects, including jewelry and semi-precious stones
Longest survival of a pig's kidney transplant: the kidney survives a month in the body of a brain-dead man in research at NYU Langone Health
Ötzi the Iceman, the 5,300-year-old mummy, has dark skin, dark eyes, and is descended from early Anatolian farmers according to results from an updated DNA study
The Northwest Territories in Canada declare a state of emergency due to wildfires and order an evacuation of the city of Yellowknife, affecting 22,000 people

US PresidentJoe Bidendesignates the site of the 1908 two-day race riot in Springfield, Illinois, as a national monument, intended to be a solemn reminder of the events which sparked the formation of the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People (NAACP)
Buddy Guy tops Billboard's Blues Albums chart with his "Ain't Done With the Blues", released on his recent 89th birthday
Rebecca Roanhorse's "Black Sun" trilogy wins the 2025 Hugo Award for Best Series at the 83rd World Science Fiction Convention, Seattle

HongwuEmperor of Ming China, Emperor Dong, hears case of couple who tore money bills while fighting over them (equal to act of destroying stamped government documents - law necessitated 100 floggings). Hongwu Emperor decides to pardon them.
Treaty of Speyer establishing Principality of Transylvania signed by King of Hungary John Sigismund Zápolyai and Holy Roman EmperorMaximilian II
PrinceEugene of Savoyoccupies Belgrade

General Hull surrenders Detroit and Michigan Territory to British forces under the command of Major General SirIsaac Brock, who captures Fort Detroit with the help of Indigenous warriors led byTecumseh

Britain'sQueen Victoriatelegraphs US PresidentJames Buchananfor the first time viatransatlantic telegraph cable; he replies, "It is a triumph more glorious, because far more useful to mankind, than was ever won by conqueror on the field of battle"
US PresidentAbraham Lincolnprohibits Union states from trading with Confederacy
War of the Triple Alliance: A Paraguayan battalion composed of children is massacred by the Brazilian Army during the Battle of Acosta Ñu
Richard Wagner's opera "Siegfried" premieres  at the Bayreuth Festspielhaus as part of the first complete performance of The Ring cycle
The French state commissions sculptorAuguste Rodinfor a large sculpted doorway "The Gates of Hell" for the proposed Musée des Arts Décoratifs
PresidentBenjamin Harrisonattends the dedication of the Bennington Battle Monument in Bennington, Vermont
The Committee of Union and Progress, "TheYoung Turks," announces a program for reforms and respect for the rights of all within the Ottoman Empire, regardless of race or religion
Emiliano ZapataandPancho Villaoverrun Mexico
Yankees sluggerBabe Ruthtees off Tommy Thomas in the 5th inning to hit the first home run out of Comiskey Park, Chicago; New York beats the White Sox 8-1
Pioneering "Blood for Britain" program sending blood plasma for WWII wounded from the US, headed byCharles R. Drew, officially begins in Britain
British Prime MinisterWinston Churchilltravels back to Cairo from Moscow
Bulgarian TsarBoris IIIvisitsAdolf Hitler
The last Chinese Emperor and ruler of the Japanese puppet state Manchukuo,Puyi, is captured by Soviet troops while preparing to board a plane to escape to Japan

Future Baseball Hall of Fame outfielderRalph Kinerhits three successive home runs for host Pittsburgh Pirates in a 12-7 win over St. Louis Cardinals; both clubs smash a then MLB record of 10 home runs
Shah of PersiaMohammad Reza Pahlaviand Princess Soraya flee to Baghdad and Rome
Martin Luther King Jr.protests for black voting rights in Miami, Florida
Ringo StarrreplacesPete Bestas the Beatles' drummer, with the first official concert two days later
The American Football League's ninth franchise is officially awarded to Joe Robbie and television starDanny Thomas; the Miami Dolphins start play in the AFL's Eastern Division in 1966
Crosby, Stills, Nash & Young perform their first public show at the Auditorium Theatre in Chicago with singer-songwriterJoni Mitchellas their opening act

King of MoroccoHassan II's Boeing 727 is attacked and shot at by six F-5 military jets during a failed coup attempt by General Mohamed Oufkir. Reportedly, King Hassan grabbed the radio and told the rebel pilots "Stop firing! The tyrant is dead!" fooling the pilots to break off the attack.
American punk rock band "TheRamones" debut in concert at CBGB in New York City
British singerPeter Gabrielannounces his departure from the rock groupGenesis
Madonna's "True Blue" album is #1 for 5 weeks, and her single "Papa Don't Preach" is #1 for 2 weeks
NYC MayorEd Kochplans to eliminate street-corner windshield washers
South African PresidentF. W. de KlerkandNelson Mandelahold emergency talks in Pretoria about increasing violence in Soweto
John Gutfreundannounces his resignation as chief executive ofSalomon Brothersamid an illegal bidding scandal
US PresidentGeorge H. W. Bushdeclares recession is near an end
British Williams driverNigel Mansellfinishes second in the Hungarian Grand Prix at the Hungaroring to clinch his first Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship
Cristiano Ronaldo(18) makes his debut for Manchester United and the Premier League in a 4–0 home victory over Bolton Wanderers
American swimmerMichael Phelpswins his seventh of eight gold medals at the Beijing Olympics when he wins the 100 m butterfly in an Olympic record of 50.58, beating Milorad Čavić of Serbia by 0.01
British sailorBen Ainslieconvincingly wins the Finn class at the Beijing Olympics, earning his third gold medal in as many Games
Jamaican sprinterUsain Boltsets a new world record of 9.69 seconds to win the 100 m gold medal at the 2008 Beijing Summer Olympics
WikiLeaksfounderJulian Assangeis granted political asylum by Ecuador
Emma Stoneis the year's highest-paid actress with $26 million, according to Forbes
Philippine police kill 32 in raids near Manila, the most deadly night in PresidentRodrigo Duterte’s war on drugs
Pope Francisand the Vatican issue a statement in support of the 300 victims of "predator priests" in Pennsylvania
Scarlett Johanssonis the world's highest-paid actress of the year, earning $40.5 million according to Forbes
Largest-ever demonstration in Belarus as 100,000 people gather in Minsk to protest against controversial election results and regime ofAlexander Lukashenko
World Snooker Championship, Crucible Theatre, Sheffield: EnglishmanRonnie O'Sullivanwins his sixth world title with an 18-8 win over countryman Kyren Wilson
US PresidentJoe Bidensays, "I stand squarely behind my decision" to withdraw US troops from Afghanistan, despite the sudden collapse of the country to the Taliban
Australian Prime MinisterAnthony Albanesereveals former PMScott Morrisonsecretly appointed himself to five ministerial portfolios between March 2020 and May 2021
Michelangelois awarded a contract to create a statue of David at Florence Cathedral by the Overseers of the Office of Works (the Operai) of the Duomo
Battle of the Spurs at Guinegate (now Enguinegatte): KingHenry VIIIof England and Holy Roman Emperor Maximilian I defeat French forces, capturing the town and routing the French cavalry
Documentary "When the Levees Broke," directed bySpike Leeabout the effects of Hurricane Katrina on New Orleans, premieres at New Orleans Arena
